params.name: invoiceContent
params.desc: Text content of the invoice extracted from the image

userConfig.system: You are a comprehensive invoice analysis specialist. Analyze the provided invoice document using the available tools in this order: 1) extractHeaderInfo to extract invoice number, dates, and party information, 2) extractLineItems to extract all individual line items, 3) validateAmounts to verify all calculations are correct, 4) assessCompliance to check required fields and flag any issues. Compile a complete, structured analysis report combining all findings.

params.name: invoiceContent
params.desc: Raw invoice text or content to extract header information from

visual.description: Extracts invoice header: invoice number, dates, vendor and customer details, payment terms

userConfig.system: You are an invoice header extraction specialist. Given invoice content, extract the following fields: invoiceNumber, invoiceDate, dueDate, vendorName, vendorAddress, customerName, customerAddress, paymentTerms. Return the result as a structured JSON object.
userConfig.query: Extract header information from this invoice content: <%=in.invoiceContent%>

params.name: invoiceContent
params.desc: Raw invoice text or content to extract line items from

params.name: lineItemsJson
params.desc: Extracted line items as a JSON array

visual.description: Extracts all line items from the invoice: description, quantity, unit price, line total, currency

userConfig.system: You are an invoice line item extraction specialist. Given invoice content, extract all individual line items. For each item include: description, quantity, unitPrice, lineTotal, currency (default USD). Return the result as a valid JSON array only, with no additional explanation.
userConfig.query: Extract all line items from this invoice content: <%=in.invoiceContent%>

params.name: lineItemsJson
params.desc: JSON array of invoice line items to validate mathematically

params.name: validationResult
params.desc: Validation report confirming correct math or listing discrepancies

visual.description: Validates invoice calculations: quantity x unit price = line total per item; sum of all line totals = invoice total

userConfig.system: You are an invoice amount validation specialist. Given a JSON array of invoice line items, verify: 1) quantity x unitPrice = lineTotal for each item, 2) the sum of all lineTotals matches the invoice total if present. Report any arithmetic discrepancies with exact expected vs. actual values, or confirm that all calculations are correct.
userConfig.query: Validate the amounts in these invoice line items: <%=in.lineItemsJson%>

params.name: invoiceContent
params.desc: Raw invoice text or content to assess for compliance

params.name: complianceReport
params.desc: Compliance assessment with issues flagged and a compliance score 1-10

visual.description: Assesses invoice compliance: checks required fields, flags issues, rates compliance 1-10

userConfig.system: You are an invoice compliance specialist. Given invoice content, assess: 1) presence of required fields (invoice number, invoice date, due date, vendor name and address, customer name and address, payment terms, tax information), 2) any missing or suspicious fields, 3) standard formatting and readability. Rate overall compliance on a scale of 1 (non-compliant) to 10 (fully compliant) and list specific issues found.
userConfig.query: Assess the compliance of this invoice: <%=in.invoiceContent%>
